objective: Establish generic Samwise squad-planning infrastructure so new product briefs can be staffed, validated, and handed off cleanly.
scopeBoundary: Create reusable planning, remediation, logging, and orchestration artifacts only; do not begin Orbit implementation in this pass.
firstCheckpoint: Land durable squad-planning logs, a role-gap remediation loop, and a reusable Samwise orchestration kit.
definitionOfDone: Every planning pass writes a durable report and schema-valid JSONL entry.
Missing or uncertain execution-critical roles route through reverse-interview and a named remediation session before execution.
Samwise has one reusable default kit covering planning, hiring, oversight, and retrospectives.

details.notes: Bootstrap planning pass records the reusable workflow itself before applying it to a product brief.

objective: Make duplicate-ID behavior guarantees explicit across the PersonaKit surfaces that matter, instead of discovering renderer behavior by surprise.
workspaceScope: PersonaKit core loader, Studio editor, and renderer-facing identity surfaces
scopeBoundary: Plan a bounded squad-led hardening pass for duplicate-ID guarantees only; do not begin execution from the protected main worktree and do not create new PersonaKit structure without AJ approval.
firstCheckpoint: Create and validate an explicit duplicate-ID guarantee envelope covering loader validation, scope precedence, editor normalization, renderer-facing collections, and targeted regressions in a dedicated non-main worktree.
definitionOfDone: Duplicate-ID guarantees are written down for the relevant loader, workspace, editor, and renderer-facing surfaces.
Renderer-facing collections no longer rely on accidental uniqueness and instead receive explicit guarantees or fail before render.
Targeted regression tests cover duplicate-ID behavior in core loading, scope precedence, and at least one Studio UI or editor path.
Build and test evidence is attached before any gate advancement.

details.executionScopeRequirement: Use a dedicated non-main worktree before samwise-worktree-squad-oversight begins.

objective: Refine assemble-squad-and-plan so any delegated subagents are given explicit PersonaKit MCP grounding requirements and robust handoff packets before execution begins.
workspaceScope: PersonaKit planning-stack artifacts for Samwise-led squad planning and execution handoff
scopeBoundary: Plan a bounded refinement of the existing planning contract, intent, directive, report template, and minimal log usage only; do not start structural pack edits in this pass.
firstCheckpoint: Refine the Samwise planning stack so delegated roles require explicit PersonaKit MCP grounding and compact handoff packets before execution handoff can be considered ready.
definitionOfDone: The planning contract requires delegated handoff packets for spawned agent roles.
The intent and directive explicitly require PersonaKit MCP grounding before delegated work begins.
The report template includes a delegated-handoff section with compact required packet fields.
Validation and stop-point language block execution when PersonaKit MCP is required but unavailable.
validationCommands: manual review that delegated handoff packets include PersonaKit MCP grounding, write scope, validation commands, and failure path fields

keyDelegatedHandoffFields: stop points and MCP failure path

objective: Make duplicate-ID behavior guarantees explicit across the PersonaKit surfaces that matter, instead of discovering renderer behavior by surprise.
workspaceScope: PersonaKit core loader, Studio editor, and renderer-facing identity surfaces
scopeBoundary: Red-pen the duplicate-ID squad plan to make delegated-agent grounding explicit: live PersonaKit MCP first, approved static export fallback second for bounded implementation or review work, otherwise stop as grounding-blocked.
firstCheckpoint: Create and validate an explicit duplicate-ID guarantee envelope covering loader validation, scope precedence, editor normalization, renderer-facing collections, delegated-lane grounding policy, and targeted regressions in a dedicated non-main worktree.
definitionOfDone: Duplicate-ID guarantees are written down for the relevant loader, workspace, editor, and renderer-facing surfaces.
Renderer-facing collections no longer rely on accidental uniqueness and instead receive explicit guarantees or fail before render.
Targeted regression tests cover duplicate-ID behavior in core loading, scope precedence, and at least one Studio UI or editor path.
Delegated execution lanes receive a compact handoff packet that names persona or session grounding, write scope, acceptance criteria, validation commands, and stop points.
PersonaKit-dependent delegated work uses a clear fallback ladder: live MCP first, approved static export second for bounded implementation or review work, otherwise stop as grounding-blocked.
Build and test evidence is attached before any gate advancement.

details.executionScopeRequirement: Use a dedicated non-main worktree before samwise-worktree-squad-oversight begins.

delegatedGroundingPolicy.fallback: Approved static PersonaKit export for bounded implementation or review work
delegatedGroundingPolicy.disallowedSilentDegrade: Planning, hiring, remediation, or open-ended discovery work must not silently degrade to cached context

objective: Refine assemble-squad-and-plan so any delegated subagents are given explicit PersonaKit MCP grounding requirements and robust handoff packets before execution begins.
workspaceScope: PersonaKit planning-stack artifacts for Samwise-led squad planning and execution handoff
scopeBoundary: Red-pen the subagent-grounding refinement plan so the delegated-lane policy is explicit: live PersonaKit MCP first, approved static export fallback second for bounded implementation or review work, otherwise stop as grounding-blocked.
firstCheckpoint: Refine the Samwise planning stack so delegated roles require explicit PersonaKit MCP grounding, approved static-export fallback rules, and compact handoff packets before execution handoff can be considered ready.
definitionOfDone: The planning contract requires delegated handoff packets for spawned agent roles.
The intent and directive explicitly require PersonaKit MCP grounding before delegated work begins.
The report template includes a delegated-handoff section with compact required packet fields.

Validation and stop-point language makes MCP-unavailable cases follow a clear fallback ladder: live MCP first, approved static export second for bounded implementation or review work, otherwise grounding-blocked.
validationCommands: manual review that delegated handoff packets include PersonaKit MCP grounding, static-export fallback rules, write scope, validation commands, and failure path fields

keyDelegatedHandoffFields: stop points and MCP failure path

delegatedGroundingPolicy.fallback: Approved static PersonaKit export for bounded implementation or review work
delegatedGroundingPolicy.disallowedSilentDegrade: Planning, hiring, remediation, or open-ended discovery work must not silently degrade to cached context

objective: Exercise the new delegated handoff fields with one bounded canary planning artifact so the planning log has a concrete example to follow.
scopeBoundary: Create one documentation-only canary planning review and matching JSONL row that use delegatedRoleNames and delegatedHandoffs without implying new execution work.
firstCheckpoint: Add one concrete canary example that proves the planning report and machine log can carry delegatedRoleNames and delegatedHandoffs together.
definitionOfDone: The planning report includes one delegated handoff packet.
The JSONL row includes delegatedRoleNames and delegatedHandoffs.
The delegated handoff packet records a valid grounding mode and failure disposition.

details.canaryPurpose: Provide one concrete example of delegatedRoleNames plus delegatedHandoffs for future planning passes.
